00030 #ifndef _TEST_H_
00031 #define _TEST_H_
00032 
00033 /*
00034  * Declarations for test code and other miscellaneous high-level
00035  * functions.
00036  */
00037 
00038 
00039 /* This is only actually available if OPT_SYNCHPROBS is set. */
00040 int whalemating(int, char **);
00041 
00042 /*
00043  * Test code.
00044  */
00045 
00046 /* lib tests */
00047 int arraytest(int, char **);
00048 int bitmaptest(int, char **);
00049 int queuetest(int, char **);
00050 
00051 /* thread tests */
00052 int threadtest(int, char **);
00053 int threadtest2(int, char **);
00054 int threadtest3(int, char **);
00055 int semtest(int, char **);
00056 int locktest(int, char **);
00057 int cvtest(int, char **);
00058 
00059 /* filesystem tests */
00060 int fstest(int, char **);
00061 int readstress(int, char **);
00062 int writestress(int, char **);
00063 int writestress2(int, char **);
00064 int createstress(int, char **);
00065 int printfile(int, char **);
00066 
00067 /* other tests */
00068 int malloctest(int, char **);
00069 int mallocstress(int, char **);
00070 int nettest(int, char **);
00071 
00072 /* Routine for running a user-level program. */
00073 int runprogram(char *progname, int nargs, char **args);
00074 
00075 /* Kernel menu system. */
00076 void menu(char *argstr);
00077 
00078 /* The main function, called from start.S. */
00079 void kmain(char *bootstring);
00080 
00081 
00082 #endif /* _TEST_H_ */
